content: Agents should declare the minimal set of tools needed. Avoid granting Write/Bash unless the agent's role requires file modification or command execution.
Agents performing code review should use permissionMode 'plan' to prevent accidental edits during analysis.
Skill bodies should follow a numbered-step runbook format: 1) Gather context, 2) Perform action, 3) Verify result. Each step should include concrete commands.
Skills should use $ARGUMENTS for user input substitution and include a usage example in the body showing the expected invocation.
CLAUDE.md should be concise: project overview, stack, development commands (test/lint/build), conventions, and gotchas. Every line should earn its place.
CLAUDE.md should list concrete commands, not placeholders. Use 'pytest tests/' not 'run tests'. Use 'ruff check --fix' not 'lint code'.
Hook events should be chosen carefully: PreToolUse for validation/gating, PostToolUse for side effects (tests, formatting), Stop for cleanup and reporting.
Hooks that run tests should use 'async: true' to avoid blocking the agent. Only use synchronous hooks for fast validation checks.
Rules should use applyTo glob patterns to scope narrowly. Broad rules slow down every prompt; targeted rules are more effective.
Commands are best for repetitive workflows: PR review, release prep, dependency updates. Use $ARGUMENTS for the variable part.
For cross-harness compatibility, avoid tool names specific to one agent platform. Use generic capability descriptions in skill bodies.
Agent description fields should explain WHEN to delegate, not just WHAT the agent does. Example: 'Use for Python backend changes in src/' not 'Python development agent'.
Security-sensitive hooks (PreToolUse on Bash/Write) should be synchronous and non-negotiable. Never use 'async: true' for security gates.
Skill allowed-tools should include Read and Grep for context gathering even when the primary action is Edit or Bash.
Generated assets should reference the project's actual test/lint/build commands detected in the repo profile, not use generic placeholder commands.
